commit.message:
fix: enable C++ exceptions for Windows ARM64 llama.cpp build

clang-cl targeting aarch64-pc-windows-msvc disables exceptions by
default, causing llama.cpp jinja code to fail with 'cannot use throw
with exceptions disabled'. The llama-cpp-sys-2 build.rs forwards
CMAKE_* env vars to CMake, so setting CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS=/EHsc fixes it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Show total elapsed time from scan start when reconnecting

When reconnecting to a running experiment via polling, the elapsed time
now shows the TOTAL duration from when the scan originally started, not
from when polling started.

Changes:
- /api/scans/<repo> endpoint now returns 'running_experiment_created_at'
  with the directory creation timestamp (when scan started)
- reconnectToRunningExperiment() accepts createdAt parameter
- Elapsed time calculated from actual scan start time using Date objects
- Progress updates show accurate 'elapsed Xm Ys' since start
- Total time on completion is now correct

Benefits:
- Users see realistic progress (e.g., 'elapsed 2m 15s' if scan ran
  that long before browser disconnected)
- Better understanding of total duration vs remaining time
- Accurate timing for 7-minute timeout threshold

Example flow:
- Scan starts at 10:00
- Browser disconnects at 10:02 (2 min elapsed)
- Browser reconnects at 10:05
- UI shows 'elapsed 5m Xs' (total since 10:00)
- Not 'elapsed 3m Xs' (time from reconnect)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add progress comments to implement lifecycle review phase

Post editable progress comments on the PR during the implement
lifecycle's internal review/fix loop:
- 🔄 Review round N/2 in progress...
- 🔧 Fixing review findings...
- ⏳ Review complete — waiting for merge...

Reuses the same comment (edit in place) across rounds to avoid
clutter. All progress updates are wrapped in contextlib.suppress
so they never block the actual work.

Closes the UX gap where the PR had no signal that a review was
running during the implement lifecycle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Improve param form and page-level error handling

fix(config): validation, writer routing, scenario persistence fixes

- Per-value validation for semicolon-separated params
- Preserve config key case through scenario save/load/export
- Preserve scenario timestamps in export/import
- Guard validate_value() against None
- Route oxygen params to bioenergetics file
- Fix plotting and ensemble edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(engine): safety and correctness fixes across engine processes

- Fix mortality output type and bioenergetics reader filenames
- Fix _collect_outputs to use pre-aggregated step_diet
- Fix numerical safety in starvation and energy budget
- Standardize error handling in movement and background processes
- Prefix unused function parameters with underscore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(migrate/registrar): eliminate dead None-arm branch in register_skips_already_registered

The `if let Some(content) = written` at line 190 of the test created a
branch whose None arm was unreachable: `register_plugins` always writes
the marketplace file even when all entries are already registered, so
`get_written` never returns None.

Replace the guarded `if let` with direct `expect()` calls (permitted in
tests per `clippy.toml`) to eliminate the dead branch and strengthen the
assertion — the test now fails explicitly if the file wasn't written.

Branch coverage: 95.45% → 95.50%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Make GP n_restarts_optimizer configurable, default 2 (P4C)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop deleting agent before deploy — preserves target_port routing

The SDK-fallback deploy path was causing 100s client_disconnect timeouts
because the PRIMARY path deleted the agent (wiping target_port=8088),
then az cognitiveservices agent create failed, and the SDK fallback
recreated the version without target_port (HostedAgentDefinition lacks
this param). Foundry then defaults routing to port 80, but the container
listens on 8088 — total port mismatch.

Changes:
- Remove destructive 'az cognitiveservices agent delete' before create
- Let create fail with 'already exists' and fall through to SDK fallback
- SDK create_version adds new version to EXISTING agent (preserving
  target_port=8088 from prior successful CLI deploys)
- Add best-effort 'agent create --target-port 8088' in SDK-fallback
  path as safety net for first-time deploys

Root cause was NOT the serve.py startup (that fix was correct) — it was
the deploy pipeline destroying agent routing config on every deploy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: rebuild AnimationStudio with correct timeline layout

- Replace broken sticky cursor (was using position:sticky with left which doesn't work as vertical line)
- Cursor now renders as position:absolute overlay on the outer panel, positioned via scrollLeft tracking — always visible above sticky elements
- Remove non-reactive getState() calls for totalDuration in TrackRow (now passed as prop)
- Fix Ruler seek: accounts for LABEL_W offset correctly
- Simplify layout: single overflow:auto scroll container with minWidth content block
- Ruler row uses position:sticky top:0, track labels use position:sticky left:0 — both work reliably in a single scroll axis container
- Auto-scroll keeps cursor in view during playback
- Clip bar shows correct width based on last keyframe time
- All keyframe lanes use reactive totalDuration prop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle browser back/forward navigation for lobby and in-game states

Use router.beforePopState to intercept browser back/forward:
- In-game → back: forfeits game and restores lobby URL
- Lobby → back: disconnects socket and resets to welcome page
- Add refs for isInGame, isLobby, room, playerName to avoid stale closures
- Track room/playerName refs in initial parse and hash change handler

commit.message:
Hide section placeholder when reconnecting to running scan

The 'Run or load a scan to view section details' placeholder was still
visible when reconnecting to a running experiment. This section-placeholder
is separate from the log output placeholder.

Now when reconnecting:

- Only the reconnect status messages are visible
- The UI clearly shows a scan is in progress

This fixes the confusing UX where multiple placeholders appeared at once.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Clear placeholder text when reconnecting to running scan

When a user selects a repo and auto-reconnects to a running experiment,
or clicks 'Watch Running Scan' from the modal, the initial placeholder
text ('Scan output will appear here...') was still visible.

Now the placeholder is cleared when reconnecting, so users see:
- Only the reconnection and status messages
- Any past output that was preserved in the DOM
- Real-time updates as polling continues

This prevents the confusing UX where both the placeholder message
and the actual scan output appear together.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert unrelated package.json and package-lock.json changes

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ythril-network/Ythril/sessions/4ba66898-1240-4c27-a8a2-076b51369e1e

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename minGiB→maxGiB (breaking), add Settings tab to Brain with space config and schema editor

Breaking API change: minGiB renamed to maxGiB across server config, API, client,
and docs. The field is a storage quota cap, not a minimum reservation.

Brain now includes a Settings tab (after Query) providing:
- Space configuration editing (label, description)
- Schema definition editing (validationMode, purpose, entityTypes, edgeLabels,
  tagSuggestions, usageNotes) with save via PATCH /api/spaces/:id

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: Ensure scan processes survive browser disconnect

Add 'start_new_session=True' to subprocess.Popen() call. This creates
the scan process in a new process group, ensuring it continues running
even if:
- The HTTP request is closed
- The user navigates away from the page
- The user refreshes the page
- The Flask server receives SIGTERM

Without this, the process would be terminated when the parent Flask
worker thread exits, killing the scan even though it was designed to
be server-persistent.

This is critical for background scans that should complete regardless
of browser connection status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prevent duplicate polling loops when reconnecting to running scan

Track polling interval in global variable currentPollInterval so we can
cancel any existing polling before starting a new one. This fixes the issue
where reconnecting multiple times would create multiple polling loops,
causing duplicate log messages.

Changes:
- Add currentPollInterval global variable
- Clear existing polling before starting new polling loop
- Set currentPollInterval to null when polling completes or times out
- Clear polling when user clicks reset button

This ensures only one polling loop runs at a time, preventing:
- Duplicate reconnect messages
- Duplicate progress updates
- Out-of-order elapsed time messages
- Resource waste from multiple simultaneous polling loops

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(keepalive): hold strong reference to ping task to prevent GC

asyncio.create_task returns a Task that the event loop holds only via a
weak reference. Without a strong reference the GC can silently discard
the task before it ever pings, making the keepalive a no-op.

Store the task in a module-level set and discard it via done_callback so
the reference is released cleanly when (if) the loop ever exits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix build error: use CSS background-image instead of for hero sections Vite resolves paths as module imports at build time, causing Rollup to fail when the image files don't yet exist in public/images/. Using inline style background-image url() is not processed by Rollup, so the build succeeds and images load at runtime when added by the client. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Unify reports into all/actionable.html with URL-param filters (#78)

* Add community, quick-wins, and consider-closing filters to unified view

Add 3 new report-mode filter checkboxes to all/actionable.html:
- Community awaiting review: is_community AND next_action matches review
- Quick wins: Ready to merge: next_action matches Ready to merge
- Consider closing: (age>90d AND stale>30d) OR (age>180d AND stale>14d)

These filters are URL-authoritative only (not localStorage-persisted) to
prevent stale state from breaking links. They compose with all existing
filters (user, area, repo, maintainer, easy-action).

Stale mode defaults to sorting by days_since_update desc. Summary bar
shows active report-mode filter names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Write redirect stubs for non-actionable reports, update index links

Build-Reports.ps1: community, quick-wins, and stale-close reports now
write lightweight redirect stubs pointing to all/actionable.html with
appropriate URL params (e.g. ?repo=runtime&community=true). The filter
predicates still run to compute counts for meta.json. Per-repo
actionable.html remains server-rendered for fast first paint.

Build-Index.ps1: community/quick-wins/consider-closing links now point
to the unified view (all/actionable.html?repo={slug}&{filter}=true).
The Most Actionable row still links to per-repo actionable.html.
Layout, counts, sparklines, and all other content are unchanged.

Nav links in per-repo actionable.html are updated to point to the
unified view for redirected report types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Pester parity tests for report filter predicates

Tests verify that community, quick-wins, and stale-close filter
predicates produce correct results with synthetic test data. Also
validates:
- Redirect stub code exists in Build-Reports.ps1
- Index link mappings in Build-Index.ps1
- JS predicates in all/actionable.html match PowerShell predicates
- Report-mode filters are URL-only (no localStorage)
- Edge cases: exact boundary values excluded (> not >=)
- Stale sort order (days_since_update descending)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix quick-wins case sensitivity, savedUser restore, improve tests

Bug fixes from rubber-duck review:
- Quick-wins JS filter now uses /Ready to merge/i (case-insensitive)
  to match PowerShell -match semantics
- Report-mode URLs (?stale=true etc) no longer restore savedUser from
  localStorage when no user= param is present, preventing shared links
  from silently becoming user-scoped views

Test improvements (22 tests, up from 16):
- Added overlapping PR categories (community+quickwin, stale+community)
- Added null field edge cases (is_community=null, next_action=null)
- Added case-insensitive quick-wins match test
- Added filter composition tests (AND semantics)
- Fixed localStorage non-persistence test to be more specific

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reorganize filter layout: checkboxes on one row, lozenges inline

Move all checkboxes (user-context, maintainer-context, and report-mode)
into a single row below the username input. Move recent user tiles
(lozenges) to the right of the username input on the same row, instead
of a separate row below. This gives a more compact, consistent layout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore recent-user lozenges to original standalone row

Move lozenges back to their own row below the username input (above
checkboxes) with original smaller/lighter styling, instead of inline
with the input controls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* UI polish: smaller input/button, simplified summary bar, involves always visible

- Match input/button font-size to checkbox labels (0.85em) with reduced padding
- Remove redundant 'Showing PRs for @X' summary bar (user visible in input above)
- Summary bar now shows only PR count/repo breakdown
- Involves checkbox always visible: checked+disabled when no user, enabled when user set
- Add spacing between lozenges and checkboxes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move scoring explainer below table, gray summary text

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move name lozenges inline after Go button

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix repo/area filter banner not showing, fix lozenge margin

- Call renderFilterBanner() after renderTable() in both renderFiltered()
  and renderFilteredMaintainer() so area/repo chips display correctly
- Remove margin-bottom from .recent-tiles (now inline in user-input row)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix clear-all/remove filter bugs, scope button CSS to avoid lozenge styling

- clearAllSecondaryFilters/removeAreaFilter/removeRepoFilter now call
  reRender() for a full re-render instead of applyTableFilter() which
  silently failed when no table existed (0-result pages)
- applyTableFilter now always updates URL and filter banner even when
  no table is present
- Scope .user-input button CSS to .user-input > button so it doesn't
  override .recent-tile button styles

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move actionable.html to docs root, all per-repo reports redirect

- Move docs/all/actionable.html to docs/actionable.html (single unified page)
- docs/all/actionable.html becomes a redirect stub preserving old bookmarks
- All index.html links now point to actionable.html?repo={slug}&...
- All per-repo reports (including actionable) are now redirect stubs
- Update CI workflow, tests, copilot instructions for new path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Sync URL bar when savedUser is restored from localStorage

When navigating to actionable.html without params, the saved user
is restored from localStorage but the URL bar stayed bare. Now
replaceState updates the URL to reflect the restored state (e.g.
?user=danmoseley) so it matches what the user would see after
manually selecting that user.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Persist full active-users list in localStorage for multi-user restore

Previously only the last single user was saved to localStorage. Now
the complete activeUsers array is saved as JSON under a new
'pr-dashboard-active-users' key. On page load without URL user params,
all saved users are restored (with backward compat fallback to the
single-user key). The URL bar is also updated via replaceState to
reflect the restored state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden redirect stubs and localStorage restore

- Validate savedUsers from localStorage: check Array.isArray and filter
  to non-empty strings, preventing corrupt data from bricking the page.
- Redirect stubs now preserve existing query params and hash from the
  old URL (e.g. /runtime/community.html?user=alice merges user=alice
  into the redirect target).
- Add 7 new Pester tests for old bookmark redirects: docs/all/ stub,
  per-repo stub URL targets, query/hash preservation, meta-refresh
  fallback, and location.replace usage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI validation for redirect stubs; HTML-encode URLs in output

- Update T5 in Test-CiValidation.ps1 to validate redirect stub
  structure (meta-refresh, location.replace, query param preservation)
  instead of checking for full report elements that no longer exist.
- HTML-encode href attributes in Build-Index.ps1 report links.
- HTML-encode meta-refresh URL in Build-Reports.ps1 redirect stubs.
- Fix misleading comment in ReportFilters.Tests.ps1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Playwright tests for unified report UI changes

- K1: involves-label is now always visible (checked+disabled when no user)
- K3-K5: summary bar shows PR count/breakdown, not filter descriptions
- K6: clear maintainer filter via checkbox uncheck, not clear link
- K7, J8: summary bar no longer includes username text
- D5: simplified filter+clear test to check row restoration only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix review comments: preserve hash in URL, fix stale test regex

- buildCurrentUrl() now includes location.hash so replaceState doesn't strip fragments
- Stale localStorage test regex checks for 'stale' not just 'staleMode'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs/actionable.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* D5: count only visible rows for meaningful filter assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ptp): rollback to working state; 1s timer, 900-sample 15min window

Roll back to commit 1440095 (one sample per tick, simple approach).
Increase poll rate from 5s to 1s for higher resolution sampling.
Expand history buffer from 180 to 900 samples (900×1s = 15min).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(#78): add reject regression test and document preceptMessage semantic rule

- Add GetClassifiedTokens_RejectString_IsPreceptMessage test (missing regression
  coverage for the reject path that was already implemented in the handler)
- Update docs/SyntaxHighlightingDesign.md to document the preceptMessage direct
  semantic token color override in semanticTokenColorCustomizations.rules, with
  rationale parallel to the preceptComment override

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor search platform modules for improved documentation and structure (#49)

- Enhanced module documentation across various search platform files, including Reddit, Semantic Scholar, StackOverflow, Wikipedia, YouTube, and SearXNG, to clarify ownership boundaries, pipeline positions, key types, and invariants.
- Updated data structures in Reddit, StackOverflow, and YouTube modules to include public field documentation.
- Improved test functions in multiple modules to return `anyhow::Result<()>` for better error handling.
- Added new `.gitkeep` files in research fixture directories to maintain directory structure.
- Refined DDG parser test to ensure at least three HTTPS non-redirect results are returned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Bot <copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
